title: Design System: Kim Roland Berame Portfolio

hello.purpose: Animated multilingual typewriter greeting; the human handshake above the hero.

headline.purpose: Section headings with a leading amber tick.

label.purpose: JetBrains Mono: nav, dates, status, tech tags, metadata.

fonts.name: mono
fonts.usage: Technical labels only; never body prose.

shadows.name: ambient-glow
shadows.purpose: The drifting background orbs (amber/teal). The room's light; persistent, behind content, never on content.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions and reveals.

motion.name: reveal
motion.purpose: Scroll reveal (opacity + 16px lift); always resolves to visible via a timeout safety net.

motion.name: orb-drift
motion.purpose: Ambient background orb rotation; per-orb varied duration.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Principal action in a region. Amber fill, ink-black text (~10:1 contrast).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: #FFB22C; color: #0A0A0A; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 10px 20px; border-radius: 10px; text-decoration: none; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #f0a526; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #FFB22C; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action. Outlined, transparent; border and text shift to amber on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: transparent; color: #DFE0E2; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.18); font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 10px 20px; border-radius: 10px; text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.2s, border-color 0.2s; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { color: #FFB22C; border-color: #FFB22C; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #FFB22C; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Tech Tag
components.description: Neutral chip with a brand-color dot. Color is signal, not fill.
components.css: .ds-tag { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 4px 10px; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1); background: #141414; border-radius: 10px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; color: #9A9DA3; } .ds-tag-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 9999px; }

components.name: Nav Link
components.description: Mono top-bar link over a translucent blurred bar. In-view section turns amber.
components.html: ProjectsExperienceAbout
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; gap: 28px; align-items: center; height: 56px; padding: 0 24px; backdrop-filter: blur(12px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(12px); background: rgba(10,10,10,0.7); border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1); } .ds-nav-link { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.8125rem; color: #9A9DA3; text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.2s; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #DFE0E2; } .ds-nav-link--active { color: #FFB22C; } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #FFB22C; outline-offset: 4px; }

components.name: Project Terminal Media
components.description: Fallback media for projects without a screenshot: a small mono terminal motif so no project ships empty.
components.html:
trimmly

$ shorten https://example.com/a/very/long/path

→ trimmly.xyz/k3a9

2,481 clicks · 99.98% uptime
components.css: .ds-term { border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1); background: #141414; border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.8125rem; } .ds-term-bar { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 12px 16px; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1); } .ds-term-bar span { width: 10px; height: 10px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #6B6E74; } .ds-term-bar em { margin-left: 8px; font-style: normal; font-size: 0.75rem; color: #6B6E74; } .ds-term-body { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; padding: 24px; color: #9A9DA3; } .ds-term-body p { margin: 0; word-break: break-all; } .ds-term .t { color: #34C7AC; } .ds-term .a { color: #FFB22C; } .ds-term .f { color: #6B6E74; }

components.name: Animated Greeting
components.description: Multilingual typewriter greeting with a blinking amber caret. The system's human handshake.
components.css: .ds-hello { font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: clamp(1.5rem, 5vw, 1.875rem); color: #9A9DA3; } .ds-hello-caret { display: inline-block; width: 2px; height: 1.1em; margin-left: 2px; background: #FFB22C; vertical-align: text-bottom; animation: ds-blink 1s steps(2) infinite; } @keyframes ds-blink { 50% { opacity: 0; } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-hello-caret { animation: none; } }

narrative.overview: A near-black canvas lit by a single warm lamp. This is the desk of an engineer building after hours: the room is dark and quiet, the work is the only thing in focus, and warmth comes from two small light sources, an amber glow and a teal glow, drifting at the edges. The personality is precise, confident, and refined, never loud. Confidence here is shown by restraint: a near-black field, generous breathing room, and color used so sparingly that when it appears it carries weight. Prose and headings ride one humanist grotesque (Hanken Grotesk) carried by weight contrast; a monospace (JetBrains Mono) handles the machine's readouts. Motion is ambient plus a subtle scroll reveal that always resolves to visible. The human notes (the multilingual greeting, the Philippines flag in the heading) are earned, specific touches against an otherwise austere frame.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black #0A0A0A body with light-ash #DFE0E2 text. Dark is the deliberate surface, not a toggle.
Two signal accents (amber, teal) used on ≤10% of any screen.
Two typefaces: Hanken Grotesk for prose, JetBrains Mono for technical labels.
Depth is tonal (bg → surface → surface-2 + hairline borders), not shadow or glass.
Ambient glow as the only persistent motion; reveals always resolve to visible.

rules.name: The Signal Rule
rules.body: Amber and teal together occupy ≤10% of any screen. They are light sources, not surfaces. The moment an accent fills a region, it has stopped being a signal.

rules.name: The One Flag Rule
rules.body: The Philippines gradient (#0038A8 -> #CE1126) appears exactly once, on 'Philippines' in the hero. It is the single sanctioned gradient-on-text because it is a flag, not decoration. Nowhere else.

rules.name: The Two Voices Rule
rules.body: Exactly two families: Hanken Grotesk for everything you read, JetBrains Mono for what the machine reports. No third typeface. Prose is never mono.

rules.name: The No-Shout Rule
rules.body: Display tops out near 3.75rem. The page never exceeds it to feel bigger; hierarchy is relative, not loud.

rules.name: The Tonal-Depth Rule
rules.body: Layering is done with the three surface tones plus hairline borders, not shadows or glass. If a panel needs to lift, raise its tone, don't float it.

dos: Do keep the body on ink black #0A0A0A with light-ash #DFE0E2 text. Dark is the committed surface, not a theme toggle.
Do confine amber #FFB22C and teal #006D5B to ≤10% of any view (The Signal Rule). Treat them as light, not paint.
Do build hierarchy with Hanken Grotesk weights (400 -> 800) and scale; reserve JetBrains Mono for labels (The Two Voices Rule).
Do layer with the three surface tones and hairline borders, not shadows or glass (The Tonal-Depth Rule).
Do keep ink-dim #9A9DA3 the floor for body text on #0A0A0A; use text-safe teal #34C7AC when teal must be read.
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion fallback, and make scroll reveals resolve to visible even if the observer never fires.
donts: Don't build the generic dev-template portfolio: hero + skill-icon grid + endless identical cards. It is a named anti-reference.
Don't drift toward a corporate SaaS landing: no buzzwords, no stock-photo enterprise tone, no hero-metric template.
Don't reintroduce glassmorphism. It was removed in the rebuild; depth is tonal.
Don't gate content visibility on a scroll animation. Reveals enhance an already-visible default and always resolve to visible.
Don't introduce a second gradient-on-text. The Philippines flag is the single sanctioned one (The One Flag Rule).
Don't add a third typeface, and don't fill tech tags with solid brand color. Color is the dot; the chip stays neutral.
Don't ship gray body text that reads under 4.5:1 on the dark field for elegance.
